---
title: "Cascading Behavior in Networks"
date: "2023-05-29"
categories: [SNA]
---

In this section, we will discuss the phenomenon of diffusion of innovation in social network. Innovation is a change of the way things are done, which can be taken by many ways, including behaviours, opinions, social norms or technologies. Therefore, our vertices would be people, seeing how innovations spread from person to person through a social network. Here, note the difference between invention and innovation. Invention is the creation of the new thing, while innovation is the dynamic process of how the invention spread through the network.

Everett Rogers(1962) started to identify some of the common principles that
applied across many different domains:
- the success of an innovation depends on its complexity for people to understand and implement;
- its observability, so that people can become aware that others are using it;
- its trialability, so that people can mitigate its risks by adopting it gradually and incrementally;
- its overall compatibility with the social system that it is entering.

## The Diffusion of Innovations

<center><img src="https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/11/Diffusion_of_ideas.svg/1200px-Diffusion_of_ideas.svg.png" alt="Diffusion of ideas.svg" width="800" height: auto></center>

### Direct-benefit effects

When a significant number of your social network connections, such as friends, acquaintances, or colleagues, start adopting a new behavior, the advantages of embracing it become more apparent to you. Rational self-interest suggests that it would be beneficial for you to adopt the new behavior once a considerable proportion of your neighbors have already done so.
